posts.raw_content:
"When vacancies happen in the representation of any State in the Senate, the executive authority of such State shall issue writs of election to fill such vacancies: Provided, That the legislature of any State may empower the executive thereof to make temporary appointments until the people fill the vacancies by election as the legislature may direct."

Notice that actually governors are not given the power to fill vacancies. They are given the power to CALL ELECTIONS to fill the vacancies. And if the STATE LEGISLATURE allows it, they can fill the vacancy. The power is given to the state legislature. Not the governor in the amendment (most states, the legislature delegates that power to the executive, which is why most states have the governor pick the replacement). Kentucky, has passed laws that do not allow the governor to fill a vacancy. So no, McConnell's replacement would not be chosen by the democrat governor of his state. It would remain vacant until a special election is held for the rest of the term.
